subject: LU-17482 llite: short read could mess up next read offset

Patch Set 1:

Bobi,

It seems like this should get a new ticket?  Since LU-16025 is closed, etc, and this is a fix to that patch.

Also, is it possible to create a test for this?

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-dne-part-3 on RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-51vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/e9d3f77f-1c14-43af-8611-c9fbd8630072 ran 3 tests.

Patch Set 1:

> It seems like this should get a new ticket?  Since LU-16025 is
 > closed, etc, and this is a fix to that patch.

Bobijam reopened that ticket... :-) Having a new ticket in Jira would be better, as well as some comment on how this issue was found (e.g. failed test case, code inspection, link to customer ticket, etc.) but landing a fix for data correctness is the priority.

 > Also, is it possible to create a test for this?

+1 for this.  We've had quite a number of fixes in this area of the code, so if we don't have a test case then it is very difficult to know if this patch improves things.  It definitely _looks_ OK, and it doesn't look to have broken any of our current tests, but those existing tests also didn't catch this issue either, so need to be improved in some way...

Does this show up with fsx, or does it need a new OBD_FAIL check, or just a specific sequence of read/write/truncate/read on multiple clients in sanityn?
Uploaded patch set 2.

parents.subject: LU-10391 lnet: support updating LNet local NI settings

commit.subject: LU-17482 llite: short read could mess up next read offset
commit.message:
LU-17482 llite: short read could mess up next read offset

When read reaches EOF, it could read data from stale pagecache, but
we need to restore the iocb->ki_pos so that next read could continue
from the correct offset.

Fixes: 4468f6c9d9 ("LU-16025 llite: adjust read count as file got truncated")
Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam@whamcloud.com>
